the_signal.headline: Context engineering now separates production agents from demos
the_signal.body: n8n's new guide on context management for LLMs covers token budgeting, context rot prevention, and prompt design that actually survives production scale. This isn't prompt optimization—it's the infrastructure layer that determines whether your agent stays reliable as it scales to real customers. GTM teams shipping agents need this figured out before your first SLA miss.

tool_drop.name: Fudge MCP
tool_drop.what: Model Context Protocol implementation for managing agent contexts and tool interactions
tool_drop.why: MCP standardization is becoming table stakes for multi-agent systems; teams building revenue automation need interop with other tools in their stack

thread_of_day.title: Agentic AI Design Patterns: From Architecture to Production
thread_of_day.takeaway: Production agents require validation layers, governance gates, error recovery circuits, and cost controls built into architecture—not bolted on after launch

quote.text: After spending the past couple of months looking at hundreds of tech docs, I tallied up 75 capabilities you'd expect an agent development tool to offer—but only half are solved
quote.source: n8n Blog: Agent Identity, Reliable Execution, and Intent

whats_moving.context: Teams are learning that persistent memory (vector stores, retrieval layers) is non-negotiable for maintaining context across multi-turn customer interactions
n8n's RAG + threat intelligence workflow pattern is emerging as the reference for SOC automation, signaling demand for compliance-grade agent workflows
Smart retry logic and circuit breakers are moving from nice-to-have to mandatory for agents handling real customer data and transactions

act_on_this: Map your agent's failure modes against the error handling classification framework in n8n's tool calling guide—identify which failures need retries vs. fallbacks vs. escalation, then wire circuit breakers before your next production deployment

numbers.stat: 75 capabilities expected from modern agent tools, but only ~37 are production-ready today
numbers.context: This capability gap is why agents built on generic LLM platforms are failing at scale—GTM teams need to audit which 25+ capabilities they're missing before committing to a customer deployment
